data.title: As a brainstorm agent, I can scan the codebase for all consumers of shared artifacts referenced in an epic's success criteria
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add Part C (shared artifact impact analysis) to the scrutiny pipeline's gap analysis step. Part C scans the codebase for all files referencing artifact(s) being created or modified by the epic, cross-references the discovered consumers against the epic's success criteria, and flags uncovered consumers.

**Why**: The scrutiny pipeline currently validates internal consistency of epic specs but does not verify external completeness — whether all consumers of a shared artifact are accounted for in the success criteria. This gap allowed epic 8df3-61d0 to ship with 4 agents covered when 7+ participate in the test quality lifecycle.

**Scope**:
- IN: Adding Part C to the gap analysis step in epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md; tech-stack-agnostic codebase scan (grep/ast-grep for path references, import statements, source/include directives); trigger logic that activates Part C only when a referenced artifact is consumed by 2+ files outside its own directory; output format as list of (file_path, matching_line, covered_by_SC: true/false) tuples; passing RAW scan results (not agent-curated summaries) to downstream consumers
- OUT: Modifying the fidelity reviewer dimensions (separate story); changes to how brainstorm invokes the scrutiny pipeline

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, Part C of the gap analysis scans the codebase for all files referencing artifact(s) being created or modified by the epic, and presents a list of (file_path, matching_line, covered_by_SC: true/false) tuples
  <- Satisfies: "The scrutiny pipeline's gap analysis includes Part C (shared artifact impact analysis)"
- When this story is complete, Part C triggers only when any SC or Approach section references creating or modifying a file that is referenced by 2+ other files outside its own directory, detected by a tech-stack-agnostic codebase scan at trigger time
  <- Satisfies: "Part C triggers when any SC or Approach section references creating or modifying a file that is referenced by 2+ other files outside its own directory"
- When this story is complete, Part C passes raw scan output (file paths and matching context) to downstream consumers, not agent-curated summaries
  <- Satisfies: "evaluated using raw Part C scan output (file paths + matching context, not brainstorm-agent summaries)"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Codebase scanning logic should use mock-compatible interfaces for unit tests — avoid hitting real filesystem in test assertions
- [Reliability] Grep/ast-grep scan may produce large output for popular artifacts — consider truncation strategy for sub-agent context limits
- [Performance] Codebase scan on large repos could be slow — consider timeout budget within the scrutiny pipeline
- [Maintainability] Part C output format is consumed by both gap analysis presentation and fidelity reviewer — establish a clear contract for the tuple format
